items.why: Students mix up gauche/droite and forget the imperative; 'à gauche' is left.
Students don't link shops to goods; bread is bought at the boulangerie.
Students confuse prepositions of place; 'en face de' means opposite, used to locate buildings.
The answer examiners want is: Opposite the station. “Inside the station” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is a building you might find in a town?
items.why: Correct answer: La mairie. “Le vélo” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
